predictions.text: At least one of Home Depot, TJX, Target, Walmart, or Lowe’s will quantify an expected FY2026 gross tariff-related cost headwind of at least $200 million (or an equivalent quantified EPS impact) in an earnings release, 10-Q/10-K, or prepared earnings-call remarks.
predictions.verification_criteria: Company disclosure (earnings release/10-Q/10-K or prepared remarks transcript) explicitly ties a quantified amount (>= $200M cost headwind or clearly equivalent EPS impact) to tariffs/import duties for FY2026.
evidence: Market coverage highlights retailer earnings focusing on tariff-driven costs and pricing/refund strategies amid ongoing uncertainty.
monitoring_signals: Earnings calendars for listed retailers
Prepared remarks PDFs and 10-Q filings mentioning “tariff,” “duties,” “import costs,” “IEEPA,” or “refunds”
Sell-side notes summarizing tariff headwinds
predictions.rationale: Retailers facing uncertain duty treatment often provide quantified headwinds to manage expectations; near-term reporting creates a natural disclosure window.

predictions.text: A new importer-led lawsuit seeking refunds of duties explicitly described as unlawful after the Supreme Court tariff-authority ruling will be filed in the U.S. Court of International Trade (CIT) or U.S. Court of Federal Claims, naming the United States and/or CBP as defendant.
predictions.verification_criteria: Publicly accessible docket entry in CIT or Court of Federal Claims showing a complaint filed by an importer/industry group seeking duty refunds/return of deposits explicitly tied to the Supreme Court ruling; complaint must reference the ruling or the invalidated tariff authority.
evidence: Reporting indicates importers are still paying tariffs impacted by the Supreme Court ruling, implying likely litigation and refund claims.
monitoring_signals: CIT and Court of Federal Claims docket searches for keywords (tariff, duties, refund, CBP, IEEPA, Supreme Court)
Trade-law firm client alerts and press releases
Industry association updates (NRF, NAM, import councils)
predictions.rationale: If duties continue being collected amid unclear refund mechanics, litigation is a common route to preserve claims and force administrative action.

predictions.text: Aggregate U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs (as tracked by a major public aggregator such as Bloomberg, BitMEX Research, or Farside Investors) will record a single-day net outflow of at least $1.5 billion.
predictions.verification_criteria: A widely cited public ETF flow dataset shows combined net outflows across U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs >= $1.5B on one calendar trading day; the figure must be explicitly reported as net flow for that day.
evidence: Recent coverage notes bitcoin falling on policy/tariff uncertainty, consistent with risk-off positioning and potential ETF redemptions.
monitoring_signals: Daily spot Bitcoin ETF flow dashboards (Bloomberg, Farside, BitMEX Research)
BTC spot volatility and large downside days
Macro risk-off indicators (VIX, USD strength)
predictions.rationale: Large one-day ETF outflows are less common than multi-day drawdowns but can occur during sharp risk-off moves.

predictions.text: At least one of Blackstone, Apollo, KKR, Ares, or Carlyle will disclose that a flagship U.S.-focused direct-lending/private-credit vehicle has a non-accrual (or equivalent impaired/credit-loss) rate of at least 3.0% by fair value, as of a quarter-end.
predictions.verification_criteria: An SEC filing, investor presentation, or earnings-call prepared remarks states non-accrual/impaired/credit-loss metric for a named private credit/direct lending vehicle and the metric is >=3.0% by fair value (or clearly comparable basis) at a specified quarter-end date.
evidence: Market commentary flags rising investor anxiety about private-credit opacity and refinancing risk, increasing likelihood of adverse credit metric disclosures.
monitoring_signals: Quarterly earnings decks and 10-Q/10-K filings for listed managers
BDC filings (10-Qs) and supplemental tables on non-accruals
Credit spreads and leveraged-loan default-rate updates
predictions.rationale: If credit stress increases, managers often provide clearer portfolio-quality metrics; a 3% threshold is meaningful yet plausible in a downturn.

predictions.text: The U.S. State Department will raise the Travel Advisory level to at least Level 3 (“Reconsider Travel”) for at least one of: United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, or Saudi Arabia, explicitly citing heightened regional conflict risk involving Iran or Iran-backed actors.
predictions.verification_criteria: Official U.S. State Department Travel Advisory page shows Level 3 or Level 4 for one listed country, and the advisory text explicitly references Iran tensions/conflict or Iran-backed/proxy threats as a driver.
evidence: U.S. officials are reported to be weighing a targeted Iran strike with escalation risk; Iran is described as preparing for war, increasing miscalculation risk.
monitoring_signals: State Department Travel Advisory updates
CENTCOM and embassy security alerts
Notices to airmen (NOTAMs) and airline rerouting announcements
predictions.rationale: Even without direct U.S.-Iran conflict, elevated threat reporting can trigger advisory upgrades for Gulf states hosting U.S. assets.

predictions.text: Mexico’s national public security system (SESNSP) will report that Jalisco’s intentional-homicide victims in a single month are at least 20% higher year-over-year versus the same month one year earlier.
predictions.verification_criteria: SESNSP monthly publication (official tables) shows Jalisco “homicidio doloso” victims for a given month and the count is >=120% of the figure for the same month in the prior year.
evidence: Reporting indicates a major cartel leader was killed, raising the risk of succession violence and localized instability.
monitoring_signals: SESNSP monthly crime statistics releases
Mexican federal security deployment announcements
Local press reports of cartel clashes in Jalisco/Michoacán
predictions.rationale: Leadership decapitation events often increase near-term fragmentation and violence; a 20% YoY monthly jump is a moderate escalation threshold.

predictions.text: The U.S. Congress will enact (bill signed into law) a Ukraine-related funding package totaling at least $20 billion in budget authority (military, economic, or combined), as stated in the law text and/or official congressional cost documents.
predictions.verification_criteria: A bill becomes law (signed) and contains Ukraine-related appropriations/authorizations with a total >= $20B, confirmed by law text, CBO cost estimate, or official congressional summaries.
evidence: Coverage notes intensified Russian attacks and continued strategic pressure on Ukraine, which can drive additional allied funding debates.
monitoring_signals: House/Senate leadership announcements and floor schedules
Bill text searches for “Ukraine” and appropriation totals
CBO scoring releases and conference report summaries
predictions.rationale: Escalating conflict intensity often renews legislative attention, but passage depends on domestic politics and budget trade-offs.

predictions.text: The U.S. Senate will hold at least one committee hearing focused primarily on AI’s influence on elections/campaigns (e.g., deepfakes, political ads, campaign funding/lobbying), where “artificial intelligence” is in the official hearing title or witness briefing topic.
predictions.verification_criteria: Official Senate committee website posts a hearing notice with AI and elections/campaign influence as the central topic, evidenced by title/agenda and archived webcast or transcript.
evidence: Reporting highlights AI funding shaping U.S. midterm politics and intensified tech lobbying, increasing likelihood of congressional oversight hearings.
monitoring_signals: Senate committee schedules (Rules, Homeland Security, Judiciary, Intelligence, Commerce)
Witness lists including AI labs, social platforms, and election officials
Released opening statements referencing campaigns/deepfakes
predictions.rationale: Election integrity and AI-generated content are recurring oversight themes; committee scheduling risk is moderate but the topic has clear salience.

predictions.text: A Phase 2 (or later) clinical trial of an oxygen-delivering gel (or oxygen gel dressing) for diabetic foot ulcers with planned enrollment of at least 100 participants will be newly registered on ClinicalTrials.gov.
predictions.verification_criteria: A new ClinicalTrials.gov registration dated on/before deadline indicates Phase 2+ study of an oxygen gel intervention for diabetic foot ulcers, with “Enrollment” (anticipated or actual) >=100.
evidence: Recent research reports suggest an oxygen gel approach could improve diabetic wound outcomes, which typically leads to scaled trials if early data is promising.
monitoring_signals: ClinicalTrials.gov new postings with keywords (oxygen gel, diabetic foot ulcer, wound oxygenation)
University/hospital press releases on translational funding
NIH RePORTER grants for DFU wound care oxygenation approaches
predictions.rationale: Promising wound-care modalities often advance to Phase 2 studies, but scale, funding, and manufacturing constraints make timelines uncertain.
